title: The 24-Hour Decision Rule
hook: Decision due? Use this 24-hour, 3-step rule to move without fear.
script: Within the next 24 hours, do three things. First, pray for wisdom and surrender the outcome to God—James 1:5 and Romans 12:2. Ask, “Lord, align my mind with Yours.” Second, open Scripture and check what clearly fits God’s character, then run it by a wise believer; Proverbs 11:14. Third, sleep once. In the morning, let Christ’s peace be the umpire—Colossians 3:15. If there’s steady peace, decide. If there’s a knot, pause or adjust. You’re not stalling—you’re obeying with clarity. Set a timer, breathe, and move forward with peace, not panic.

title: Stop Overthinking: Decide in a Day
hook: You may be overthinking this—here’s a 24-hour way to know God’s will.
script: Two job offers? A hard text to send? Try this. Tonight, pray it out loud and hand God the steering wheel. Ask for wisdom and a renewed mind—James 1:5, Romans 12:2. List what honors Jesus in each option. Tomorrow, read verses that speak to integrity and purpose, then call one mature Christian for counsel; Proverbs 11:14. After that, stop researching. Sleep once. In the morning, check your heart: does Christ’s peace sit steady? Colossians 3:15. If both choices honor God, you have freedom—choose and give thanks. Peace first, choice second. Save this as your go-to rhythm.

title: When God Feels Silent: 24-Hour Clarity
hook: If God feels silent about your decision, try this before you spiral.
script: Silence doesn’t mean abandonment; it’s an invitation to trust. Here’s your 24-hour clarity rule. Pray honestly and surrender control; ask for wisdom and a renewed mind—James 1:5, Romans 12:2. Search Scripture for what’s clearly right or wrong, then seek one voice of counsel; Proverbs 11:14. Now, rest. God designed your brain to reset in sleep, and He leads with peace, not panic. After one night, let the peace of Christ rule your heart—Colossians 3:15. Move with that peace. If it fades later, course-correct—grace is bigger than a wrong turn. God guides moving feet. Start the clock and breathe.
